_meta.description: EXAMPLE MCP server configuration templates for The Librarian
_meta.contract: EXAMPLE ONLY — copy values into your OpenWork MCP extension settings or local OpenWork config. Do not commit machine-local values.
_meta.platform_separation_note: macOS and Windows examples are kept separate. No single command works on both platforms. Pick your platform's example.

examples.label: macOS — Swift-run Librarian Server (stdio bridge)
examples.note: Requires Swift toolchain. Uses the mcp-bridge.sh stdio wrapper.

examples.label: macOS — stdio bridge script (reuse mcp-bridge.sh)
examples.note: Alternative: run the shell bridge directly. Reads JSON-RPC from stdin and proxies to the remote Librarian MCP endpoint.

examples.label: Windows — compiled LibrarianServer.exe (stdio bridge)
examples.note: The Librarian Swift server compiled for Windows as LibrarianServer.exe. Replace <repo-root> with your actual repository path. Do not commit machine-local values.

examples.label: Windows — PowerShell stdio bridge (reuse mcp-bridge.ps1)
examples.note: PowerShell equivalent of the macOS mcp-bridge.sh. Reads JSON-RPC from stdin and proxies to the Librarian MCP endpoint. Replace <repo-root> with your actual repository path.

examples.label: Windows — PowerShell stdio bridge (repo-relative, from librarian-runtime-node)
examples.note: Same as above, but referencing mcp-bridge.ps1 relative to librarian-runtime-node. Replace <runtime-node-root> with your local clone path.

platform_key.macOS_only: This example will NOT work on Windows. Requires macOS + Swift toolchain.
platform_key.windows_native_command: This example is Windows-native and will NOT work on macOS without WSL or equivalent.

placeholders.<repo-root>: Replace with the full path to your cloned TheLibrarian repository, e.g., C:\Users\<user>\Projects\TheLibrarian
placeholders.<runtime-node-root>: Replace with the full path to your cloned librarian-runtime-node repository, e.g., C:\Users\<user>\Projects\librarian-runtime-node
